finding: Official Shimano specs support FC-U6000-2 36-22T, CS-LG400-11 11-45T, RD-U6020-11 capacity closure and FD-U6000 front capacity for a 2x11 CUES drivetrain.
Official Shimano specs support a 2x12 MTB combination using 36-26T front gearing with a 10-45T cassette under the relevant Shimano 2x MTB derailleur capacity envelope.
Official Shimano specs support trekking 3x9 configurations including 44-32-22T front gearing, 11-34T cassette, RD-T3000 capacity closure and FD-T3000 front capacity.
Official Shimano specs support trekking 3x10 configurations including 48-36-26T front gearing and 11-36T cassette under the relevant trekking derailleur capacity envelope.
Official Shimano specs support a traditional MTB 3x10 example using 40-30-22T front gearing and 11-36T cassette under the relevant Shimano MTB 3x derailleur capacity envelope.
Official Shimano specs support 10-51T MTB 12-speed cassette configurations. For a 1x drivetrain, total range is set by cassette spread when the chainring is fixed.
calculation_or_scope: range=51/10=510.0%; absolute min/max ratios require a selected chainring and are not claimed here

finding: Official Shimano specs support a common 105 2x road configuration using 50-34T front gearing and 11-34T cassette under the relevant Shimano road derailleur capacity envelope.
Official Shimano specs support a GRX 2x gravel configuration using 48-31T front gearing and 11-34T cassette under the relevant Shimano GRX derailleur capacity envelope.
Official SRAM Eagle product page identifies a 10-52T cassette configuration. For a 1x drivetrain, total range is set by cassette spread when the chainring is fixed.
calculation_or_scope: range=52/10=520.0%; absolute min/max ratios require a selected chainring and are not claimed here

finding: Official SRAM XPLR product page identifies a 10-44T cassette configuration. For a 1x drivetrain, total range is set by cassette spread when the chainring is fixed.
calculation_or_scope: range=44/10=440.0%; absolute min/max ratios require a selected chainring and are not claimed here

finding: Official Shimano Alfine SG-S7001-11 page identifies the 11-speed internal-gear hub and its 409% total gear range.
calculation_or_scope: Hub-internal range=409%; whole-bike final drive ratios depend on external sprocket/chainring selection

finding: Official Pinion P-line page states P1.18 has 18 gears and 636% range, and P1.12 has 12 gears and 600% range.
calculation_or_scope: Use as gearbox-internal range; whole-bike final drive ratios depend on external sprocket/chainring selection

finding: Official Pinion E-Drive MGU page lists E1.9 as 9 gears and 568% range, and E1.12 as 12 gears and 600% range.
calculation_or_scope: Use as MGU internal transmission range; absolute internal min/max ratios are not captured in current project evidence

finding: Official Rohloff SPEEDHUB specifications list 14 gears, 13.6% increments and 526% total range; the official internal ratio table spans roughly 0.279 to 1.467.
calculation_or_scope: Hub-internal range=526%; whole-bike final drive ratios depend on external sprocket/chainring selection

finding: Avinox MG Concept Tech Details booth graphic shows infinitely variable gear ratio with a displayed 100%-520% range across 1-13 virtual gears.
calculation_or_scope: Relative displayed range=520%; booth/OCR evidence, not final public datasheet; internal mechanism not confirmed

finding: Gobao X-system official page lists Urban & Trekking at 400% max gear range and MTB & Cargo at 500% max gear range.
calculation_or_scope: Official page product blocks; direct X1/X1P naming and commercial readiness still require closure

finding: Official E2Drives technology page lists first-generation Propulsive Module claims including 265% ratio range and use on Decathlon LD920E.
calculation_or_scope: Official marketing/technology page; use as Gen 1 city-product claim rather than high-power eMTB/cargo validation

finding: Transition Velo reports Owuru Ride as a prototype motor with integrated continuous variation and 500% variation range.
calculation_or_scope: Media first ride/prototype evidence; not an official high-power datasheet

finding: Official enviolo products page describes Automatic/CVP products in the 310-400% range and lists Utility at 400%, Trekking v4 at 380% and Extreme Gearbox at 380%.
calculation_or_scope: Hub/gearbox route, not motor-internal MGU; product-specific support guides should be checked before engineering limits
